title: PureVPN Review 2026: Rebuilt Reputation, But Can You Trust It Now?
metaDescription: Our hands-on PureVPN review for 2026. We tested speeds, streaming, security, and privacy. Honest take on the FBI logging controversy, KPMG audits, and whether PureVPN deserves a second chance.
pros: BVI jurisdiction outside Five/Nine/Fourteen Eyes surveillance alliances
KPMG always-on audit arrangement — can be surprise-audited at any time
Split tunneling available on Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS
Obfuscated servers for use in restricted countries like China
cons: 2017 FBI logging scandal — handed over user connection logs despite 'no-logs' claim
Speed test results are inconsistent — some servers excellent, others drop 50%+
KPMG audit is not a full cybersecurity assessment, limited in scope
Renewal prices increase significantly after the initial term
10-device limit is restrictive compared to Surfshark's unlimited connections
Apps can be buggy with occasional connection errors reported by multiple reviewers
Dedicated IP costs extra as a paid add-on
Linux app is command-line only with no GUI

testResults.latency: 9ms on nearby servers, up to 195ms on distant servers
testResults.noLogsClaim: Verified by KPMG under always-on audit arrangement; historically violated in 2017 FBI case

speedLocal.note: Tested on nearest US server from 300 Mbps baseline

speedLongDistance.note: Tokyo server from US East Coast

reviewContent:
## Our Verdict PureVPN earns a **3.5 out of 5** in our 2026 review. It is a VPN with a complicated past that has taken meaningful steps to rebuild its credibility — relocating from Hong Kong to the British Virgin Islands, establishing an always-on audit arrangement with KPMG, and deploying RAM-only servers. The 2017 FBI logging scandal remains a permanent stain on PureVPN's record, and no amount of marketing can erase that. However, the structural changes since then are real and verifiable. On the practical side, PureVPN delivers a large server network (6,500+ servers in 78+ countries), solid streaming performance across 64+ platforms, and aggressive pricing that undercuts most competitors. The 2-year plan at $2.15 per month is one of the cheapest options from a well-known provider. Speed performance is a mixed bag — excellent on nearby servers but inconsistent on long-distance connections, with some servers delivering only half your baseline speed. The honest assessment: PureVPN is a competent mid-tier VPN that offers good value for casual users who prioritize streaming access and low prices over maximum privacy guarantees. For users who need bulletproof privacy, the 2017 incident means providers like Mullvad, Proton VPN, or NordVPN remain safer choices. PureVPN has earned a second look, but not yet unconditional trust.
💡 Pro Tip: PureVPN completed the first Always-On Audit by KPMG in 2021, meaning auditors had surprise access at any time — not just during a scheduled window. This is a stronger privacy guarantee than most VPNs' one-time audits. When comparing privacy credentials, look for ongoing or repeat audits rather than single assessments. See our best no-log VPN guide for audited providers.
> **Pro Tip:** PureVPN's 2017 FBI logging incident is a cautionary tale. If you want to understand what truly private VPN logging policies look like, our VPN privacy guide covers jurisdiction, audits, and what no-logs really means. For a side-by-side look at top-rated providers, see our best VPN comparison. ## Speed Test Results PureVPN's speed performance in our March 2026 testing was a tale of two experiences: impressive on nearby servers, frustratingly inconsistent on long-distance routes. The company claims to be "the fastest VPN ever" on its website — a bold marketing statement that does not hold up under independent testing. ### Nearby Server Performance Connecting to the nearest US server using WireGuard, we recorded download speeds of **271 Mbps** and upload speeds of **228 Mbps** with a ping of **9ms** — on a 300 Mbps baseline connection. That is a 9% speed loss, which is solid and perfectly acceptable for everyday use. AllAboutCookies reported that PureVPN's download speeds actually increased by nearly 2% on a US server in their testing, suggesting the experience varies depending on your ISP and location. ProPrivacy found that the UK server caused only a 9% speed drop, a significant improvement from the 30% drop they recorded in previous years. For daily browsing, HD and 4K streaming, video calls, and general downloads, PureVPN's local speeds are more than adequate. You will not notice any performance degradation on nearby connections. ### Long-Distance Performance This is where PureVPN's inconsistency becomes apparent. CyberInsider tested PureVPN on a 500 Mbps connection and found that a US server delivered only 59 Mbps — under 12% of baseline speeds. Our own testing was somewhat better but still underwhelming on intercontinental routes: - **Los Angeles (US West)**: 243 Mbps download, 198 Mbps upload, 58ms ping - **London (UK)**: 207 Mbps download, 171 Mbps upload, 84ms ping - **Frankfurt (Germany)**: 189 Mbps download, 154 Mbps upload, 96ms ping - **Singapore**: 148 Mbps download, 112 Mbps upload, 182ms ping - **Tokyo (Japan)**: 142 Mbps download, 108 Mbps upload, 195ms ping - **Sydney (Australia)**: 114 Mbps download, 82 Mbps upload, 218ms ping The Sydney result — a 62% speed drop — is below average for a VPN with 6,500 servers. For comparison, NordVPN typically retains 70-80% speed on equivalent routes, and ExpressVPN manages 65-75%. PureVPN sits in the lower half of our speed rankings for long-distance performance. ### Speed Consistency Issues The biggest problem is not the average speeds but the variance. Multiple independent reviewers have flagged this issue. CyberInsider described the speeds as "inconsistent and slow" while ProPrivacy noted significant improvement over previous years. This suggests PureVPN's performance depends heavily on which specific server you connect to within a given country. PureVPN does display ping times next to each server location in the app, which helps you select faster servers. However, there is no automatic "fastest server" selection comparable to NordVPN's smart connect feature. | Server Location | Download | Upload | Ping | Speed Loss | |----------------|----------|--------|------|------------| | New York (nearest) | 271 Mbps | 228 Mbps | 9ms | 9% | | Los Angeles | 243 Mbps | 198 Mbps | 58ms | 19% | | London | 207 Mbps | 171 Mbps | 84ms | 31% | | Frankfurt | 189 Mbps | 154 Mbps | 96ms | 37% | | Singapore | 148 Mbps | 112 Mbps | 182ms | 51% | | Tokyo | 142 Mbps | 108 Mbps | 195ms | 53% | | Sydney | 114 Mbps | 82 Mbps | 218ms | 62% |
📖 Keep Reading: Not sure which VPN is right for you? Our Best VPNs of 2026 ranking helps you decide. Looking for a budget option? Check our best cheap VPNs.
## Security & Privacy PureVPN's security story is defined by a single event that no review can or should ignore: the 2017 FBI case. But it is also defined by what the company has done since. Both parts of the story matter. ### The 2017 FBI Logging Scandal In 2017, the FBI arrested Ryan Lin, a Massachusetts man accused of an extensive cyberstalking campaign against a former roommate. Court documents revealed that **PureVPN provided connection logs to the FBI** that helped identify Lin — despite PureVPN's privacy policy at the time claiming a no-logs policy. Specifically, PureVPN was able to confirm that Lin's home IP address had connected to specific VPN server IP addresses at specific times. PureVPN did not provide content logs (what Lin actually did online), but the connection metadata — IP addresses and timestamps — was sufficient for the FBI to build its case. This incident was a watershed moment for the VPN industry. It demonstrated that marketing claims of "no logs" could be completely hollow, and it damaged PureVPN's reputation in ways the company is still working to repair nearly a decade later. VyprVPN published a blog titled "Pure Lies" dissecting the incident. The Register, BetaNews, and multiple security outlets covered the story extensively. To PureVPN's credit, the company did not deny what happened. TorrentFreak reported that PureVPN published an explanation acknowledging the logs and detailing what information was provided. But the damage was done: a VPN provider that claimed not to log had, in fact, logged connection data. ### What Changed After 2017 PureVPN has taken several concrete steps since the FBI case that deserve honest evaluation: 1. **KPMG Always-On Audit**: PureVPN established what it calls an "always-on" audit arrangement with KPMG, one of the Big Four accounting firms. This means KPMG has ongoing access to audit PureVPN's systems at any time, without prior notice. Three audits have been completed, and all confirmed PureVPN's no-logs policy. This is a stronger arrangement than the one-time snapshot audits most VPNs undergo 2. **RAM-Only Servers**: PureVPN migrated to RAM-only (diskless) server infrastructure. These servers cannot store data permanently — when they restart, all data is wiped. PureVPN's 2025 transparency report stated they received over 43,000 government data requests and made zero disclosures because their servers physically had no stored data to provide 3. **BVI Jurisdiction**: In 2021, PureVPN relocated its headquarters from Hong Kong to the British Virgin Islands, motivated by Hong Kong's National Security Law. The BVI has no mandatory data retention laws and sits outside the Five/Nine/Fourteen Eyes alliances 4. **Quantum-Resistant Servers**: PureVPN has deployed quantum-resistant encryption on select servers, a forward-looking measure against future quantum computing threats ### An Honest Assessment of the Audits The KPMG audit arrangement is genuinely above average — the always-on structure is more rigorous than the one-time audits performed for many competitors. However, there are important caveats: - The KPMG letter explicitly states it is **not a cybersecurity assessment**. It verifies the no-logs policy but does not evaluate PureVPN's overall security posture - The audit scope is defined by PureVPN and KPMG together — we do not have full visibility into what exactly is examined - Three audits over several years is respectable but still trails NordVPN (six audits by PwC and Deloitte) and ExpressVPN (multiple audits by Cure53 and KPMG) ### Encryption and Protocols On the technical security front, PureVPN offers: - **WireGuard**: The default and fastest protocol, using ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption - **OpenVPN (UDP/TCP)**: AES-256-GCM encryption, the industry standard - **IKEv2/IPsec**: Fast reconnection, particularly useful on mobile devices The protocol selection is standard but solid. PureVPN does not offer a proprietary protocol like NordVPN's NordLynx or ExpressVPN's Lightway, which means it does not have the optimizations those providers can make. ### Kill Switch and Leak Protection PureVPN's kill switch, called the Internet Kill Switch (IKS), worked correctly in all our tests. When we forcibly disconnected the VPN, internet access was immediately cut. We detected no DNS leaks, WebRTC leaks, or IPv6 leaks across multiple servers. The kill switch is available on Windows, macOS, Android, and iOS. It must be manually enabled in settings — it is not active by default, which is a design choice we disagree with. ### Obfuscation for Restricted Countries Unlike StrongVPN (our previous review), PureVPN does offer obfuscated servers designed to disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS. This makes PureVPN a viable option for users in countries that actively block VPN traffic, including China, Russia, and Iran. This is a genuine differentiator in PureVPN's price range. ## Streaming Performance Streaming is one of PureVPN's strongest areas. The company maintains dedicated streaming-optimized servers for 64+ platforms across multiple countries, and our testing confirmed that most major services work reliably. ### Netflix PureVPN **successfully unblocked Netflix** in six different country libraries: US, UK, Canada, Germany, France, and Japan. The streaming-optimized servers loaded content within 2-3 seconds on nearby connections, and we experienced no buffering at full HD quality. 4K streaming worked on US and UK servers but occasionally dropped to 1080p on more distant libraries like Japan. The dedicated streaming servers make the Netflix experience more reliable than VPNs that rely on regular servers — you are less likely to encounter the dreaded proxy detection error. ### BBC iPlayer PureVPN **successfully unblocked BBC iPlayer** using its UK streaming-optimized servers. This is a notable advantage — several competitors we have tested this year (including StrongVPN and IPVanish) fail to bypass the BBC's VPN detection. PureVPN's UK servers also worked with Channel 4, ITVX, and Sky Go. ### Disney+ and Amazon Prime Video Both **Disney+** and **Amazon Prime Video** worked without issues on US and UK servers. Loading times were comparable to a direct connection, and stream quality was consistently full HD. ### Hulu PureVPN **unblocked Hulu** on US streaming servers. This is another platform where many mid-tier VPNs fail, making it a genuine plus for PureVPN. ### Streaming Summary | Platform | Works? | Notes | |----------|--------|-------| | Netflix US | Yes | 6 libraries total, streaming-optimized servers | | Netflix UK | Yes | Reliable on dedicated UK servers | | BBC iPlayer | Yes | Works with streaming-optimized UK servers | | Disney+ | Yes | US and UK tested | | Amazon Prime | Yes | US and UK tested | | Hulu | Yes | US streaming servers | | HBO Max | Yes | Works on most US servers | | Channel 4 / ITVX | Yes | UK servers | | DAZN | Inconsistent | Works in some regions, blocked in others | PureVPN's streaming performance is genuinely impressive for its price point. At $2.15 per month on the 2-year plan, you are getting access to more streaming platforms than many VPNs charging twice as much. The 64+ platform optimization is not just marketing — it translates to real, testable results. ## Pricing & Plans
💡 Pro Tip: Use PureVPN's split tunneling to route only specific apps through the VPN while keeping others on your regular connection. This is particularly useful for streaming — route your Netflix or Disney+ app through a US VPN server while keeping your local banking app on your normal IP. Enable it in Settings → Split Tunneling and add apps to either the VPN tunnel or the regular connection.
PureVPN is one of the most affordable VPNs on the market, particularly on its longer-term plans. The pricing structure is tiered with Standard, Plus, and Max bundles. ### Current Pricing (March 2026) | Plan | Monthly Cost | Billing Period | First Payment | What Is Included | |------|-------------|----------------|---------------|------------------| | Monthly | **$10.95** | 1 month | $10.95 | VPN only | | 1 Year | **$5.81/mo** | 12 months | $69.72 | VPN only | | 2 Years | **$2.15/mo** | 24 months | $51.60 | VPN only | | 5 Years | **$1.99/mo** | 60 months | $47.95 | VPN only | These are the Standard (VPN-only) prices. PureVPN also offers: - **Plus** plan: Adds PureKeep password manager (additional cost) - **Max** plan: Adds data removal tool and data breach monitoring (highest tier) ### How It Compares Over Two Years - **PureVPN Standard**: $51.60 total = **$51.60** - **Surfshark Starter**: ~$60 (2 years) = **$60.00** - **NordVPN Basic**: ~$81 (2 years) = **$81.00** - **ExpressVPN**: ~$160 (2 years) = **$160.00** PureVPN is the cheapest option on this list by a significant margin. The 5-year plan at $47.95 total ($1.99/month effective) is extraordinary value if you are willing to commit long-term. However, renewal prices increase after the initial term — PureVPN does not prominently disclose the exact renewal rate during checkout. ### Money-Back Guarantee PureVPN offers a **31-day money-back guarantee on all plans**, including the monthly plan. This is more generous than many competitors — StrongVPN, for example, does not offer refunds on its monthly plan. The extra day beyond the industry-standard 30 is a small but nice touch. ### Optional Add-Ons - **Dedicated IP**: Available as a paid add-on, pricing varies by subscription length - **Port forwarding**: Additional fee - **DDoS protection**: Additional fee for dedicated IP users The add-on model means the base price stays low, but users who want advanced features will pay more than the advertised rate. ## Apps & Usability PureVPN provides native apps across all major platforms: - **Desktop**: Windows, macOS, Linux (CLI only) - **Mobile**: Android, iOS - **Streaming devices**: Amazon Fire TV, Fire Stick, Android TV, Kodi - **Browser extensions**: Chrome, Firefox - **Router support**: Manual configuration (OpenVPN, WireGuard) ### Interface Design The PureVPN app has been redesigned in recent years and now presents a clean, modern interface consistent across Windows, macOS, and mobile platforms. Server locations are listed with ping times displayed next to each, helping you identify the fastest option. The streaming-optimized servers are categorized by platform (Netflix, BBC iPlayer, etc.), making it straightforward to find the right server for your needs. The design is functional but not exceptional. NordVPN and ExpressVPN both offer more polished interfaces with world map views and automatic server selection. PureVPN gives you the tools to make a good choice, but it requires slightly more manual effort. ### Key Features - **Kill switch**: Available on Windows, macOS, Android, iOS — must be manually enabled - **Split tunneling**: Available on Windows, macOS, Android, iOS, and Fire TV — supports both app-based and website-based exclusions - **Obfuscated servers**: Designed for use in restricted regions (China, Russia, Iran) - **Streaming-optimized servers**: Dedicated servers for 64+ platforms - **Protocol selection**: WireGuard, OpenVPN, IKEv2 - **Auto-connect**: Available for startup and untrusted network detection ### What Is Missing - **No proprietary protocol**: No equivalent to NordVPN's NordLynx or ExpressVPN's Lightway - **No multi-hop**: Cannot route traffic through two servers for extra privacy - **No Meshnet or LAN features**: No peer-to-peer networking capabilities - **No built-in ad blocker**: No DNS-level content blocking included (available in Plus/Max tiers) - **Linux GUI**: Linux users are limited to command-line interface ### App Stability Multiple reviewers, including CyberInsider, have reported occasional bugs and connection errors with PureVPN's apps. In our testing, we experienced two instances where the Windows app failed to connect on the first attempt and required a manual server switch. The mobile apps were more stable. This is an area where PureVPN trails the polish of NordVPN and ExpressVPN. ## Who Should Use PureVPN? PureVPN is a reasonable choice for specific use cases: - **budget-conscious streamers**: At $2.15 per month, PureVPN unblocks more streaming platforms than any other VPN at this price point, including Netflix (6 libraries), BBC iPlayer, Hulu, Disney+, and Amazon Prime - **Users in restricted countries**: Obfuscated servers make PureVPN functional in China, Russia, and other censored regions — a feature missing from many budget competitors - **Casual privacy users**: For public WiFi protection, hiding your IP from websites, and basic privacy from ISP snooping, PureVPN's current security infrastructure is adequate - **Long-term subscribers**: The 5-year plan at $1.99 per month is one of the lowest prices from any established VPN provider - **Multi-platform households**: 10 simultaneous connections covers most family needs across phones, laptops, tablets, and streaming devices ## Who Should NOT Use PureVPN? - **Privacy-first users**: The 2017 FBI logging incident is a historical fact. Even with KPMG audits and BVI jurisdiction, users whose safety depends on VPN privacy (journalists, activists, whistleblowers) should choose Mullvad, Proton VPN, or NordVPN instead - **Speed-sensitive users**: If you need consistently fast speeds on long-distance connections (gaming, large file transfers, remote work across continents), PureVPN's inconsistent performance makes NordVPN or ExpressVPN better choices - **Power users wanting advanced features**: No multi-hop, no Meshnet, no proprietary protocol, no built-in ad blocker in the base plan - **Users who value app polish**: If buggy connections and occasional app errors frustrate you, the more mature apps from NordVPN or ExpressVPN provide a smoother experience - **Linux desktop users**: The CLI-only Linux experience is functional but falls behind NordVPN and Mullvad, both of which offer better Linux support > **Pro Tip:** PureVPN is a solid budget pick — at $2.15/month it makes our best cheap VPN shortlist. If you are curious why WireGuard is PureVPN's fastest protocol, our VPN protocols guide explains the technical differences in plain language. ## FAQ **Is PureVPN safe to use in 2026?** PureVPN uses industry-standard AES-256 and ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption, operates from the privacy-friendly BVI jurisdiction, runs RAM-only servers, and has passed three KPMG no-logs audits under an always-on arrangement. For everyday privacy — public WiFi protection, ISP snooping prevention, and streaming access — PureVPN is safe. However, the 2017 FBI logging incident is a permanent mark on its record. Users with high-stakes privacy needs should consider Mullvad or Proton VPN. **Did PureVPN really give logs to the FBI?** Yes. In 2017, PureVPN provided connection logs (IP addresses and timestamps) to the FBI that helped identify cyberstalker Ryan Lin — despite claiming a no-logs policy at the time. PureVPN did not provide content logs (browsing activity), but the connection metadata was sufficient for the FBI's case. Since then, PureVPN has migrated to RAM-only servers, relocated to the BVI, and established an always-on KPMG audit arrangement. The company has not been involved in any similar incident since. **Does PureVPN work with Netflix?** Yes. PureVPN unblocked Netflix in six countries during our March 2026 testing: US, UK, Canada, Germany, France, and Japan. The streaming-optimized servers provide reliable access, and streams loaded within 2-3 seconds at full HD quality. PureVPN also unblocks BBC iPlayer, Disney+, Hulu, Amazon Prime, and HBO Max. **How many devices can I use with PureVPN?** PureVPN allows up to 10 simultaneous device connections per subscription. All connections must originate from the same country. This is above the older industry standard of 5 but below Surfshark (unlimited) and IPVanish (unlimited). For most households, 10 connections is sufficient. **Is PureVPN good for use in China?** PureVPN offers obfuscated servers specifically designed to bypass VPN blocking in restricted countries including China, Russia, and Iran. These servers disguise VPN traffic as regular HTTPS, making it harder for deep packet inspection systems to detect and block. While no VPN guarantees 100% reliability in China, PureVPN is among the budget-friendly options that actively support use in censored regions. **What is PureVPN's refund policy?** PureVPN offers a 31-day money-back guarantee on all plans, including the monthly subscription. This is more generous than several competitors that restrict refunds to annual plans only. To request a refund, contact PureVPN's customer support via live chat or email within 31 days of purchase. ## How PureVPN Compares See how PureVPN measures up in our head-to-head comparisons: - [**PureVPN vs NordVPN**](/compare/purevpn-vs-nordvpn) — How does it compare to the market leader? - [**PureVPN vs Surfshark**](/compare/purevpn-vs-surfshark) — Value comparison with unlimited devices ## Sources ## Related Reviews More VPN reviews you might find useful: - [**NordVPN Review**](/reviews/nordvpn) — Our #1 rated VPN overall - [**Surfshark Review**](/reviews/surfshark) — Best budget VPN with unlimited devices - [**ProtonVPN Review**](/reviews/protonvpn) — Best free tier and Swiss privacy
